B.name: Avocado Toast
steps: Toast 2 slices of sourdough until golden and crisp.
Halve avocado, scoop flesh into bowl. Mash with fork, add lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper.
Spread generously on toast. Top with chilli flakes.
Optional: add a fried or poached egg on top.
B.tip: Everything bagel seasoning on top is a game changer.

L.name: Greek Salad with Chickpeas
steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas well.
Dice cucumber, quarter cherry tomatoes, thinly slice red onion.
Combine chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, olives and onion.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt and pepper for dressing.
Toss everything, crumble feta on top.
L.tip: Marinate red onion in lemon juice 10 min — removes the raw sharpness.

D.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl
steps: Cook quinoa and let cool slightly.
Roast chickpeas with olive oil, cumin and paprika at 200°C for 20 min.
Dice cucumber, halve tomatoes, chop parsley.
Assemble: quinoa base topped with all vegetables, chickpeas and crumbled feta.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice. Spoon hummus on the side.
D.tip: Warm pita bread on the side makes this more filling.

B.name: Besan Chilla
steps: Mix besan with turmeric, cumin, ajwain, salt and enough water to make a smooth, pourable batter — like pancake batter.
Add finely chopped onion, tomato, green chilli and grated ginger. Mix well.
Heat a flat non-stick pan on medium. Brush lightly with oil.
Pour one ladle of batter, spread in a circle. Drizzle oil around edges.
Cook 3 min until edges lift and bottom is golden. Flip and cook 2 min more. Serve with curd.
B.tip: The batter should be smooth — no lumps. Add water slowly and whisk well. Rest the batter 5 min for better chillas.

L.name: Chana Masala
steps: Cook chickpeas until tender (canned works — drain well).
Blend onion, tomatoes, ginger and garlic to smooth paste.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add paste, cook until oil separates ~8 min.
Add spices, cook 2 min. Add chickpeas + 1 cup water, simmer 10 min.
Finish with garam masala and amchur.
L.tip: A tea bag while boiling dried chickpeas gives rich dark colour.

D.name: Spinach & Feta Frittata
steps: Preheat grill to high. Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan (no plastic handle).
Sauté onion and capsicum 3 min. Add garlic, cook 30 sec. Add spinach until wilted.
Beat 6 eggs with salt and pepper. Pour over vegetables in the pan.
Cook on medium heat without stirring for 3 min until bottom is set.
Dot cherry tomatoes and crumble feta on top. Grill 3–4 min until top is golden and set. Rest 2 min before slicing.
D.tip: A frittata is great cold the next day — make it for dinner and take a slice to work for lunch.

B.name: Bread Upma
steps: Tear bread into rough 2cm pieces. No need to remove crusts.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, peanuts and curry leaves — cook until seeds pop and peanuts are golden.
Add onion and green chilli. Cook 3 min until onion is soft.
Add chopped tomato and turmeric. Cook 2 min until tomato softens.
Add bread pieces, toss well to coat in masala. Season with salt. Cook 2 min on medium heat. Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with coriander.
B.tip: Use slightly stale bread — it holds up better and does not turn mushy when tossed with the masala.

L.name: Dal Tadka
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and salt until mushy. Mash and adjust.
Heat ghee — add cumin, asafoetida, red chillies — sizzle 30 sec.
Add garlic golden, then onion golden, then tomato soft.
Pour tadka over dal, stir, simmer 5 min.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or roti.
L.tip: Use real ghee for the tadka — the flavour difference is dramatic.

D.name: Dal Fry with Jeera Rice
steps: Pressure cook both dals with turmeric and salt until completely soft. Whisk smooth.
Heat ghee in a separate pan. Add cumin seeds, dried red chillies, asafoetida — sizzle 20 seconds.
Add very finely chopped onion, cook 5 min until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.
Add diced tomato, cook until oil separates. Pour this tarka into the dal.
For jeera rice: heat ghee, add cumin, add soaked basmati, cook 12 min on low with 1.5x water covered.
D.tip: Dal fry should be thicker and richer than dal tadka — use less water and let it reduce more.

B.name: Masala Moong Sprouts
steps: If using raw sprouts, microwave with 2 tbsp water for 90 seconds until just tender but still with bite.
Finely chop onion, tomato, cucumber and green chilli.
Mix sprouts with chopped vegetables in a bowl.
Add grated ginger, lemon juice, chaat masala and salt. Toss well.
Garnish with fresh coriander. Eat immediately or refrigerate up to 4 hours.
B.tip: Sprouts can be pre-germinated over 2 days from moong dal — soak overnight, drain, rinse twice daily. Or buy ready sprouts from the supermarket.

L.name: Palak Paneer
steps: Blanch spinach 1 min, ice-water shock, blend smooth.
Sauté onion, garlic, ginger golden. Add tomato, cook soft. Blend smooth.
Return blended mix to pan. Add spinach puree, simmer 5 min.
Add pan-fried paneer cubes. Cook 3 min.
Swirl in cream. Serve with roti.
L.tip: Ice water after blanching keeps spinach vibrant green.

D.name: Roasted Cauliflower Steak
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C. Slice cauliflower into 2cm thick steaks from the centre.
Brush both sides generously with olive oil mixed with smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder and salt.
Roast on a tray 20 min, flipping halfway. Should be deeply golden at the edges.
Toss chickpeas in same spices, add to tray for last 10 min until crispy.
Serve cauliflower steak on tahini sauce, topped with crispy chickpeas, parsley and pomegranate seeds.
D.tip: Cut through the core — it holds the steak together. Outer florets will fall apart and can be roasted separately.

B.name: Overnight Oats
steps: Add ½ cup oats to a jar.
Pour over ¾ cup milk, add 2 tbsp Greek yogurt and 1 tbsp chia seeds.
Drizzle with honey. Stir well. Seal and refrigerate overnight.
Top with fresh berries and banana slices before eating.
B.tip: Prep 5 jars on Sunday — breakfast sorted all week.

L.name: Chole with Jeera Rice
steps: Use canned or pre-cooked chickpeas. Sauté onion until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook 2 min.
Add blended tomato and chole masala. Cook until oil separates — about 4 min.
Add chickpeas with ¼ cup water. Simmer 10 min until thick and saucy.
For jeera rice: heat ghee, add cumin seeds, add soaked basmati rice, cook 12 min covered on lowest heat.
Serve chole over jeera rice with coriander, lemon and sliced onion.
L.tip: Canned chickpeas work perfectly here — saves 45 minutes without compromising taste. Drain and rinse well.

D.name: Cheesy Veggie Pasta Bake
steps: Cook pasta until 2 min before al dente — it will finish in the oven.
Sauté onion, garlic, mushrooms and capsicum in olive oil until soft. Add spinach until wilted.
Add crushed tomatoes and Italian herbs. Simmer 5 min.
Toss pasta with sauce in a baking dish. Top generously with mozzarella and parmesan.
Bake 200°C for 15 min until cheese is golden and bubbling. Rest 5 min before serving.
D.tip: Undercook the pasta — it keeps cooking in the oven and absorbs sauce. Overcooked pasta bake is mushy.

B.name: Masala Dosa
steps: For filling: boil and roughly mash potatoes. Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves. Sauté onion, green chilli, ginger until soft. Add turmeric, mix in potatoes. Garnish with coriander.
Heat a flat tawa until very hot. Splash a little water — it should sizzle away.
Pour one ladle of dosa batter. Spread immediately in concentric circles into a thin disc.
Drizzle oil around the edges. Cook until the edges lift and the dosa is golden and crispy.
Place potato filling on one half, fold and serve immediately with coconut chutney and sambar.
B.tip: The tawa must be very hot and well-seasoned. A cold tawa gives thick, soft dosas instead of crispy ones.

L.name: Pulihora
steps: Soak tamarind in warm water, extract thick pulp. Heat in a pan with turmeric and salt until it thickens — about 5 min.
Heat oil until smoking hot. Add mustard seeds, dried red chillies, curry leaves, asafoetida — sizzle vigorously.
Add peanuts and fry until golden. Add green chilli.
Pour the entire tempering over the tamarind paste. Mix well — this is the pulikaarapu.
Add cooked and cooled rice. Mix gently but thoroughly. Taste and adjust salt and tamarind. Garnish with sesame seeds.
L.tip: The rice must be completely cool before mixing — hot rice turns mushy. Make pulikaarapu ahead and mix rice just before eating.

D.name: Matar Paneer
steps: Blend onion, tomato, ginger and garlic to smooth paste.
Heat oil, add cumin. Add paste, cook until oil separates ~7 min.
Add spices. Cook 2 min. Add peas + ½ cup water. Cover 5 min.
Add paneer cubes. Simmer 4 min.
Garnish with coriander. Serve with roti.
D.tip: Golden-fry paneer cubes first for better texture and colour.

B.name: Chole Bhature
steps: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas until very soft — 4 whistles. Reserve cooking liquid.
For chole: make a thick masala with blended onion, tomato, ginger, garlic. Cook until oil separates. Add chole masala, amchur, chickpeas. Simmer 15 min until thick.
For bhature: mix flour, curd, baking soda, salt and oil. Knead into soft dough. Rest 30 min.
Roll bhature slightly thicker than roti. Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden — about 90 seconds each.
Serve immediately — bhature deflate quickly.
B.tip: Bhature must be served the instant they come out of the oil. They are best eaten within 2 minutes.

L.name: Pesara Pappu with Rice
steps: Wash moong dal. Pressure cook with turmeric and salt — 2 whistles. It should be soft and slightly mashable.
Heat ghee in a pan. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves. Add finely chopped onion, cook 3 min.
Add ginger, green chilli, tomato. Cook until tomato is soft — about 3 min.
Add cooked moong dal. Mix well. Simmer 5 min. Adjust consistency — should be slightly thick.
Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with steamed rice, papad and mango pickle.
L.tip: Moong dal cooks quickly — do not overcook or it loses texture. It should hold shape but be completely tender.

D.name: Paneer Stuffed Paratha
steps: Knead soft wheat dough with water and salt. Rest 10 min.
Mix grated paneer with green chilli, ginger, cumin, coriander, red chilli, salt — keep dry.
Roll dough disc, add filling in centre, seal edges, roll gently into paratha.
Cook on hot tawa, apply ghee both sides until golden spots appear ~4 min.
Serve with curd and pickle.
D.tip: Grate paneer fine — coarse filling tears the dough when rolling.

steps: Dry roast semolina in a pan on medium heat until lightly golden and fragrant steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas. Toss with cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt and olive oil.
